title: David A. Patterson | EECS at UC Berkeley
content: David A. Patterson | EECS at UC Berkeley David A. Patterson: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo David A. Patterson Professor Emeritus Contact Information 579 Soda Hall tel: 642-6587 fax: 643-7352 pattrsn@cs.berkeley.edu Office Hours Mondays, by appointment, 579 Soda Links Personal Homepage Publications Dissertations Research Support Amy Frithsen frithsen@berkeley.edu Biography David Patterson is the Pardee Professor of Computer Science, Emeritus at the University of California at Berkeley, which he joined after graduating from UCLA in 1976. Dave's research style is to identify critical questions for the IT industry and gather inter-disciplinary groups of faculty and graduate students to answer them. The answer is typically embodied in demonstration systems, and these demonstration systems are later mirrored in commercial products. In addition to research impact, these projects train leaders of our field. The best known projects were Reduced Instruction Set Computers (RISC), Redundant Array of Inexpensive Disks (RAID), and Networks of Workstations (NOW), each of which helped lead to billion-dollar industries A measure of the success of projects is the list of awards won by Patterson and as his teammates: the ACM A.M. Turing Award, the NAE Charles Stark Draper Prize for Engineering, the BBVA Foundation Frontiers of Knowledge Award, the C & C Prize, the IEEE von Neumann Medal, the IEEE Johnson Storage Award, the SIGMOD Test of Time award, the ACM-IEEE Eckert-Mauchly Award, and the Katayanagi Prize. He was also elected to both AAAS societies, the National Academy of Engineering, the National Academy of Sciences, the Silicon Valley Engineering Hall of Fame, and to be a Fellow of the Computer History Museum. The full list includes about 50 awards for research, teaching, and service. In his spare time, he co-authored seven booksâincluding two with John Hennessy, who is past President of Stanford University and with whom he shared the Turing Award, the Draper Prize, and several othersâPatterson also served as Chair of the Computer Science Division at UC Berkeley, Chair of the Computing Research Association, and President of ACM. He is currently Board Chair of Laude Foundation and a distinguished engineer at Google Deepmind.
